deep_dive.title: SafeGen: LLM-Driven Assertion Generation and Fault Criticality Evaluation for Functional Safety
deep_dive.abstract: With advances in autonomous driving and electric vehicle technologies, functional safety has become a critical requirement in automotive chip design. Traditional simulation-based fault analysis is often overly conservative at the module level and fails to accurately reflect fault criticality. This paper presents SafeGen, an LLM-driven, formal-verification-assisted framework for functional-safety-oriented fault criticality assessment. SafeGen leverages large language models (LLMs) and a document-level Hyper Knowledge Graph (HyperKG) that incorporates Failure Modes, Effects, and Diagnostic Analysis (FMEDA) guidelines to extract verifiable specifications from design and safety documents and evaluate their relevance to overall system safety. The HyperKG is further enriched with register-transfer-level (RTL) information to guide the generation of Functional Safety Assertions (FSAs) that are both semantically grounded and design-aware. Each assertion is linked to its corresponding specification, enabling traceable reasoning throughout the assessment process. A gate-to-RTL fault-mapping mechanism supporting both stuck-at and bridging faults, combined with formal property verification (FPV), enables semantic-level fault criticality grading based on specification-linked assertion violations. A digital-physical co-simulation platform for a field-oriented control (FOC) system is developed to validate SafeGen. Experimental results demonstrate that SafeGen generates higher-quality assertions than existing LLM-based assertion generation frameworks while providing greater semantic interpretability in fault criticality assessment compared with traditional simulation-based approaches.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM-driven assertion generation for hardware functional safety
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: LLM驱动的硬件功能安全断言生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: LLM驱动的硬件功能安全断言生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: LLM驱动的硬件功能安全断言生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: SafeGen uses LLMs and formal verification to generate assertions and evaluate fault criticality for automotive chip functional safety.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文提出SafeGen框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）和形式验证来生成可验证的规约并评估故障关键性。该方法从设计文档和安全指南中提取规约，结合超知识图谱进行推理，通过形式验证提高精度。在汽车芯片设计场景中，SafeGen显著提升了故障分析的准确性，展示了LLM在硬件形式验证中的实用价值。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文提出SafeGen框架，利用大语言模型（LLM）和形式验证来生成可验证的规约并评估故障关键性。该方法从设计文档和安全指南中提取规约，结合超知识图谱进行推理，通过形式验证提高精度。在汽车芯片设计场景中，SafeGen显著提升了故障分析的准确性，展示了LLM在硬件形式验证中的实用价值。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: large language model for hardware formal verification

deep_dive.title: Evidence-Driven LLM Agent for C-to-Synthesizable-C Conversion and Verification
deep_dive.abstract: Software-compilable C programs routinely fail to complete the four-stage pipeline of a high-level synthesis (HLS) toolchain -- compilation, C simulation (CSim), synthesis, and C/RTL co-simulation (CoSim) -- because HLS accepts only a synthesizable subset of C (HLS-C). Yet most existing large language model (LLM) systems built for HLS code repair only cover the early pipeline stages and feed raw tool logs directly to the model, yielding brittle and hard-to-reproduce fixes. We formulate C-to-HLS-C conversion as a closed-loop generation-verification-diagnosis-repair problem on an HLS tool (Xilinx Vitis), contributing three components: an end-to-end workflow of cooperating agents closed by the four-stage verifier under strict evidence isolation; a Progressive Mismatch Localization Chain (PMLC) that localizes CSim/CoSim mismatches through log normalization, AST backward slicing, and dual-trace instrumentation; and a typed-query, two-stage evidence RAG backed by a self-evolving, family-routed repair-card pool. Experimental results show that the proposed workflow substantially outperforms all comparable state-of-the-art models.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: LLM agent for C-to-synthesizable-C conversion and verification in HLS flow
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: LLM智能体用于C到可综合C的转换及HLS流程中的验证
deep_dive.llm_evidence: LLM智能体用于C到可综合C的转换及HLS流程中的验证
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: LLM智能体用于C到可综合C的转换及HLS流程中的验证
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an evidence-driven LLM agent that uses a closed-loop generation-verification-diagnosis-repair workflow with Xilinx Vitis to convert C to HLS-C, achieving better HLS toolchain pass rates.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对软件C程序难以通过HLS工具链四个阶段（编译、C仿真、综合、C/RTL协同仿真）的问题，提出了一种证据驱动的LLM智能体系统。该系统在Xilinx Vitis平台上实现闭环生成-验证-诊断-修复，通过四个阶段的严格验证反馈迭代修正代码，相比直接使用工具日志的方法显著提高了HLS工具链通过率。该工作展示了LLM在硬件设计验证中的具体应用，尤其适用于RTL级验证场景。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对软件C程序难以通过HLS工具链四个阶段（编译、C仿真、综合、C/RTL协同仿真）的问题，提出了一种证据驱动的LLM智能体系统。该系统在Xilinx Vitis平台上实现闭环生成-验证-诊断-修复，通过四个阶段的严格验证反馈迭代修正代码，相比直接使用工具日志的方法显著提高了HLS工具链通过率。该工作展示了LLM在硬件设计验证中的具体应用，尤其适用于RTL级验证场景。

quick_skim.title: On Parameterized Verification Over Tree Topologies
quick_skim.abstract: Parameterized verification of finite-state processes with rendez-vous synchronization is notoriously undecidable when processes are linearly ordered. In this paper we study two kinds of bounds under which we determine the complexity of safety checking over tree topologies. When bounding the depth we obtain that the complexity is related to the fast growing hierarchy. Our second bound limits the alternations between upwards and downwards synchronizations in the tree (phases), and occurs naturally in many concrete settings. If we fix the number of phases then the complexity of safety checking is EXPSPACE complete, and if the number of phases is part of the input it is 2EXPSPACE complete (both for arbitrary depth).
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Parameterized verification technique applicable to hardware systems
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 适用于硬件系统的参数化验证技术
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 适用于硬件系统的参数化验证技术
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 适用于硬件系统的参数化验证技术
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies complexity of safety checking for parameterized verification over tree topologies under two bounds, showing EXPSPACE and 2EXPSPACE completeness, providing transferable methods for hardware verification of tree-structured systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对树形拓扑上参数化验证的安全性检查问题，本文在深度有界和相位交替有界两种条件下给出了复杂度结果。通过限制进程间同步的交替次数，证明了当相位数量固定时复杂度为EXPSPACE完全，相位数量作为输入时为2EXPSPACE完全。该工作为树形结构硬件系统的形式验证提供了可迁移的理论方法，对多核处理器或片上网络的验证具有潜在价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对树形拓扑上参数化验证的安全性检查问题，本文在深度有界和相位交替有界两种条件下给出了复杂度结果。通过限制进程间同步的交替次数，证明了当相位数量固定时复杂度为EXPSPACE完全，相位数量作为输入时为2EXPSPACE完全。该工作为树形结构硬件系统的形式验证提供了可迁移的理论方法，对多核处理器或片上网络的验证具有潜在价值。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索混合硬件验证方法以及可迁移到硬件验证的软件验证技术。, especially work that connects or combines: hardware verification techniques for digital circuits; symbolic execution applied to hardware design verification; fuzzing techniques for hardware verification; hybrid verification combining formal and simulation methods; counterexample-guided inductive synthesis for hardware verification; abstract interpretation applied to hardware verification; What papers combine formal verification with simulation for hardware verification?; How are software verification techniques like symbolic execution and fuzzing adapted to hardware design verification?; What are the hybrid methods that integrate model checking with emulation or runtime verification for digital circuits?.

quick_skim.title: Structural Certification for Reliable Physical Design with Language Models
quick_skim.abstract: An unreliable language model can be made to produce reliable physical designs if the authority to assert is moved out of the model: the model proposes, and a deterministic engine alone certifies, returning certified, impossible, or unknown. We introduce Physics-Anchored Certification (PHACT), a propose-certify loop spanning five scientific domains, and identify what makes such a certificate trustworthy. A checker that accepts a model-supplied value can be forged; deriving the certified quantity from fixed inputs instead makes forgery impossible by construction. Across eighty adversarial trials spanning two models, two decoding temperatures, and a deliberately faulted engine, this contract produced zero false certifications.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Language model for reliable physical design certification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 语言模型用于物理设计可靠性认证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 语言模型用于物理设计可靠性认证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 语言模型用于物理设计可靠性认证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Physics-Anchored Certification (PHACT), a propose-certify loop using language models to propose designs and deterministic engines to certify, achieving zero false certifications in 80 adversarial trials across five domains, ensuring reliable physical design.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 语言模型在物理设计生成中常产生不可靠输出，现有认证机制易被伪造。本文提出物理锚定认证（PHACT），通过提议-认证循环将断言权从模型移出：语言模型提议设计，而确定性引擎基于固定输入推导不可伪造的认证量。在跨越五个科学领域的80次对抗试验中，即使引擎故意有缺陷，也实现了零错误认证。该方法为语言模型在物理设计中的可靠应用提供了通用框架。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 语言模型在物理设计生成中常产生不可靠输出，现有认证机制易被伪造。本文提出物理锚定认证（PHACT），通过提议-认证循环将断言权从模型移出：语言模型提议设计，而确定性引擎基于固定输入推导不可伪造的认证量。在跨越五个科学领域的80次对抗试验中，即使引擎故意有缺陷，也实现了零错误认证。该方法为语言模型在物理设计中的可靠应用提供了通用框架。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Papers central to 搜索关于大语言模型在硬件形式验证中应用的论文。, especially work that connects or combines: large language model for hardware formal verification; hardware formal verification using large language models; RTL verification with LLMs; What are the latest advances in applying LLMs to formal verification of RTL designs?; Can LLMs assist in model checking and theorem proving for hardware design?.

quick_skim.title: Modal CEGAR-tableaux with RECAR and resolution-based SAT-shortcuts
quick_skim.abstract: We investigate two approaches for extending CEGAR-tableaux with SAT-shortcuts using a previously known approach called RECAR but also a totally new approach using the modal resolution theorem prover KSP  as an oracle. Our experiments using our C++ implementation CEGARBox++ of CEGAR-tableaux show that:   (1) CEGARBox++ with RECAR SAT-shortcuts is not competitive   (2) CEGARBox++ using KSP to provide SAT-shortcuts is superior to both CEGARBox++ and KSP,   particularly on large satisfiable problems.   As far as we know, this is the first effective integration of SAT, tableaux and resolution methods  for modal satisfiability which performs better than its parts.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: CEGAR-tableaux method is transferable to hardware formal verification
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: CEGAR-tableaux方法可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.llm_evidence: CEGAR-tableaux方法可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: CEGAR-tableaux方法可迁移至硬件形式验证
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper improves modal satisfiability checking by integrating CEGAR-tableaux with SAT-shortcuts via resolution, offering a transferable method for formal verification tasks like hardware model checking.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对模态可满足性问题提出了一种新的CEGAR-tableaux扩展方法，通过结合RECAR和基于谓词消解（KSP）的SAT捷径，解决了传统表推演方法在大规模可满足问题上的效率瓶颈。实验表明，集成KSP的CEGARBox++在大型可满足问题上显著优于单独使用CEGARBox++或KSP。该工作首次有效地整合了SAT、表推演和消解方法，为包括硬件形式验证在内的诸多领域提供了可迁移的验证技术。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对模态可满足性问题提出了一种新的CEGAR-tableaux扩展方法，通过结合RECAR和基于谓词消解（KSP）的SAT捷径，解决了传统表推演方法在大规模可满足问题上的效率瓶颈。实验表明，集成KSP的CEGARBox++在大型可满足问题上显著优于单独使用CEGARBox++或KSP。该工作首次有效地整合了SAT、表推演和消解方法，为包括硬件形式验证在内的诸多领域提供了可迁移的验证技术。
